req.title: Vital signs validation (audit)
req.statement: An Observation with category vital-signs shall have value in clinically plausible range; out-of-range shall be flagged for review. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.
expected_outcomes: flag raised if out of range

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates three concerns: (1) valid vital-signs Observations are stored, (2) out-of-range values are flagged and a review task is created, and (3) an AuditEvent is emitted for every operation. Test design covers: EP for a normal in-range heart-rate (positive), NEG for a physiologically impossible value (negative), BVA for the exact upper boundary of the plausible range (boundary). A fourth DT test covers the AuditEvent emission rule orthogonally, and a fifth STC test walks the full state machine from Observation creation through flag-raised to review-task-created, satisfying end-to-end audit traceability. All tests are self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own resources.

tests.name: test_inrange_vitalsigns_observation_stored
actions: POST /Observation with category=vital-signs, code=heart-rate, valueQuantity=72 bpm
Assert HTTP 201 and Location header returned
GET the returned Location
Assert no review flag present on the resource
expected: No flag or review extension on the resource
AuditEvent with action='C' emitted for this Observation id
trace: R-HLT-094: vital-signs shall have value in clinically plausible range
R-HLT-094: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_inrange_vitalsigns_observation_stored(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, cleanup_resource, assert_audit_event):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 72, "unit": "bpm", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    obs_id = resp.json()["id"]
    cleanup_resource("Observation", obs_id)

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}", headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    body = get_resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "final"
    extensions = body.get("extension", [])
    flag_urls = [e["url"] for e in extensions]
    assert not any("review" in u or "flag" in u for u in flag_urls), "Unexpected review flag on in-range observation"

    assert_audit_event(entity_id=obs_id, action="C")

tests.name: test_outofrange_vitalsigns_creates_review_flag
preconditions: value 999 bpm is outside any plausible range
actions: POST /Observation with category=vital-signs, code=heart-rate, valueQuantity=999 bpm
Assert HTTP 201 or 422 depending on server policy
If 201: GET Observation and assert review flag extension is present
Assert a Task resource flagging the Observation for review exists
Assert AuditEvent emitted with action='C' and outcome indicating flag
expected: Server accepts or rejects with documented status code
If stored: Observation carries a review-flag extension
A Task with intent=order referencing the Observation id is created
trace: R-HLT-094: out-of-range shall be flagged for review
R-HLT-094: error pathway - review task created
R-HLT-094: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_outofrange_vitalsigns_creates_review_flag(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, cleanup_resource, assert_audit_event, search_resources):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "preliminary",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 999, "unit": "bpm", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (201, 422), f"Unexpected status {resp.status_code}"
    if resp.status_code == 422:
        pytest.skip("Server rejects out-of-range at ingestion; review flag pathway not applicable")

    obs_id = resp.json()["id"]
    cleanup_resource("Observation", obs_id)

    body = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}", headers=clinician_auth_headers).json()
    ext_urls = [e["url"] for e in body.get("extension", [])]
    assert any("review" in u or "flag" in u for u in ext_urls), "Review flag extension missing for out-of-range value"

    tasks = search_resources("Task", {"focus": f"Observation/{obs_id}"})
    assert len(tasks) >= 1, "No review Task created for out-of-range Observation"
    assert tasks[0]["intent"] == "order"

    assert_audit_event(entity_id=obs_id, action="C")

tests.name: test_boundary_max_heartrate_200bpm_accepted_without_flag
preconditions: heart-rate upper boundary of plausible range is exactly 200 bpm (inclusive)
actions: POST /Observation with category=vital-signs, code=heart-rate, valueQuantity=200 bpm
Assert HTTP 201
GET Observation and assert no review flag
Assert AuditEvent emitted
trace: R-HLT-094: value in clinically plausible range
R-HLT-094: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
BVA: upper boundary value is included in the valid partition
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_boundary_max_heartrate_200bpm_accepted_without_flag(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, cleanup_resource, assert_audit_event):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 200, "unit": "bpm", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at upper boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    obs_id = resp.json()["id"]
    cleanup_resource("Observation", obs_id)

    body = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}", headers=clinician_auth_headers).json()
    assert body["status"] == "final"
    ext_urls = [e["url"] for e in body.get("extension", [])]
    assert not any("review" in u or "flag" in u for u in ext_urls), "Boundary value 200 bpm should NOT trigger review flag"

    assert_audit_event(entity_id=obs_id, action="C")

tests.name: test_auditevent_emitted_for_read_operation
preconditions: an existing Observation with id known to the test via fixture
actions: GET /Observation/{id} to perform a read operation
Assert HTTP 200
Query AuditEvent search endpoint filtering on entity.what=Observation/{id} and action=R
Assert at least one AuditEvent record exists for the read
expected: AuditEvent with action='R' and entity referencing the Observation id is present
trace: R-HLT-094: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end
DT: every operation type (C/R/U/D) must appear in AuditEvent log
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_auditevent_emitted_for_read_operation(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, existing_observation_id, assert_audit_event):
    # existing_observation_id fixture creates a known Observation and yields its id
    obs_id = existing_observation_id

    read_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}", headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert read_resp.status_code == 200, f"GET failed: {read_resp.status_code}"

    # Allow eventual-consistency propagation
    import time; time.sleep(1)

    assert_audit_event(entity_id=obs_id, action="R")

    audit_search = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Observation/{obs_id}", "action": "R"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert audit_search.status_code == 200
    bundle = audit_search.json()
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "No AuditEvent found for READ operation"
    agent_names = [a.get("who", {}).get("display", "") for e in entries for a in e["resource"].get("agent", [])]
    assert any(agent_names), "AuditEvent agent not populated"

tests.name: test_state_transition_observation_to_reviewed
preconditions: heart-rate value 250 bpm is out of plausible range
system auto-creates a review Task on out-of-range detection
actions: POST /Observation with valueQuantity=250 bpm to trigger flag (state: flagged)
Assert review Task exists with status=requested
PUT /Task/{task_id} with status=completed to mark reviewed (state: reviewed)
GET /Observation/{obs_id} and assert review extension is resolved or status updated
Assert AuditEvent emitted for each state-changing operation
expected: Observation transitions from preliminary/flagged to reviewed state
Task transitions from requested to completed
AuditEvent recorded for Observation POST and Task PUT
No orphaned flag remains after Task completion
trace: R-HLT-094: out-of-range shall be flagged for review
R-HLT-094: error pathway - review task created
R-HLT-094: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end
STC: covers flagged -> under-review -> reviewed state transitions
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_state_transition_observation_to_reviewed(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, cleanup_resource, assert_audit_event, search_resources):
    obs_payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "preliminary",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 250, "unit": "bpm", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    post_resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=obs_payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    obs_id = post_resp.json()["id"]
    cleanup_resource("Observation", obs_id)

    tasks = search_resources("Task", {"focus": f"Observation/{obs_id}", "status": "requested"})
    assert len(tasks) >= 1, "Review Task not created for out-of-range Observation"
    task = tasks[0]
    task_id = task["id"]
    cleanup_resource("Task", task_id)

    task["status"] = "completed"
    put_resp = requests.put(f"{base_url}/Task/{task_id}", json=task, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert put_resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Task update failed: {put_resp.status_code}"

    assert_audit_event(entity_id=obs_id, action="C")
    assert_audit_event(entity_id=task_id, action="U")

    final_task = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Task/{task_id}", headers=clinician_auth_headers).json()
    assert final_task["status"] == "completed", "Task did not reach completed state"
